message.content: {"applicable": true, "component_key": "method-trialing", "title": "Unterbaue Methodenprüfung mit autonomer Codeexploration", "motivation": "Shadow-Frog demonstriert, dass Agenten durch aktive Erkundung unerforschter Codebereiche wertvolles Wissen zur Fehlererkennung und Feature-Ideation aufbauen – diese Erkenntnisse lassen sich direkt in die Generierung von Bestehenskriterien und das Auffinden von Ruhestandskandidaten für Jonis Methoden überführen.", "desired": "Erweitere das method-trialing-Modul um eine 'Shadow-Frog'-Komponente, die in Leerlaufphasen selbstständig Experimente auf unterrepräsentierten Codeabschnitten ausführt und die gewonnenen Bugs, Inkonsistenzen oder neuen Szenarien als automatisch generierte Testfälle speichert, mit denen Methoden bewertet und veraltete Verfahren identifiziert werden.", "acceptance": "Innerhalb einer Woche generiert die Komponente mindestens fünf neue, valide Bestehenskriterien (z.B. Testfälle), die keine manuelle Eingabe erforderten, und schlägt mindestens zwei Methoden mit nachweislich veralteten oder fehlerhaften Ergebnissen für den Ruhestand vor."}
message.reasoning_content:
We are asked: could this source concretely improve one of Joni's non-core modules exactly? The source is about Shadow-Frog, a system that autonomously explores a codebase to build knowledge for coding agents. It runs experiments on underexplored parts and captures learnings. This might be relevant for modules like method-trialing (harvesting methods, running trials? maybe the idea of autonomous discovery loops could improve how method-trialing works, e.g., automatically exploring the codebase to discover new method candidates). But we need to be precise: the module allowedlist includes method-trialing, which involves pass-criterion and retirement of methods. Could Shadow-Frog's active exploration loops improve how we trial/retire methods? Possibly by autonomously running experiments on the codebase to test methods? But Shadow-Frog is about exploring a codebase to discover knowledge, not specifically about method discovery or retirement. However, the phrase "shadow knowledge base" and "autonomous discovery loops" might map onto the idea of an emergent strengthening layer: candidates earn development through some process. But the source is specifically about codebase knowledge for retrieval, bug detection, and ideation. Not obviously about how a system's own methods get validated. So maybe not applicable.

Alternatively, reader-sources: Shadow-Frog could improve the reading layer by providing a new kind of feed (the shadow knowledge base) of discovered codebase insights? But the reading layer is about which feeds Joni can draw on; Shadow-Frog is about creating a knowledge base from idle time. Could be a new source if Joni were to use such a memory. But the module reader-sources says "which feeds Joni can draw on" - if we interpreted it as adding a feed from Shadow-Frog, but that would be a new external source, not directly implementing Shadow-Frog. Shadow-Frog is a system that would produce a knowledge base; Joni could possibly incorporate that as a source. So maybe reader-sources: add a feed that imports from a Shadow-Frog knowledge base. But the source itself is a description of a system, not a plug-and-play module. However, we could say it's a method to automatically generate a knowledge base from the codebase, and then Joni's reading layer could draw on that. That seems plausible but stretched: the source is about a whole agent system, not a modular improvement to a reading layer. It would require Joni to integrate the whole Shadow-Frog system as a new source, but the module is about adding a feed, not building a whole agent. So maybe not.

Conflict-qualifier: not relevant.

Emergence: how candidates earn development. Shadow-Frog's discovery loops could maybe generate new candidate features/improvements? But emergence is about strengthening existing candidates, not discovering new ones.

Method-trialing: the pass-criterion and retirement of methods. Could Shadow-Frog's autonomous experiments be used to trial methods? Possibly if Joni's method-trialing module could autonomously explore codebase to generate test cases for methods? But the source describes exploring underexplored parts of the codebase to capture learnings; that might be a way to discover edge cases that could be used as pass-criteria. So maybe method-trialing could adopt a "shadow knowledge base" to generate new criteria for retiring methods. That would be a concrete change: the method-trialing module could automatically generate test scenarios from underexplored code regions. So: applicable = true, component_key = "method-trialing", title: "Unterbaue Methodenprüfung mit autonomer Codeexploration", motivation: "Shadow-Frog zeigt, dass Agenten in Leerlaufphasen unerforschte Codeabschnitte untersuchen und dabei Wissen sammeln, das die Fehlererkennung und Feature-Ideen verbessert – dieses Prinzip kann auf die Generierung von Bestehenskriterien und das Auffinden von Ruhestandskandidaten in Joni übertragen werden.", desired: "Erweitere das method-trialing-Modul um eine 'Shadow-Frog'-Komponente, die in Inaktivitätsfenstern automatisch Experimente auf ungenutzten Codebereichen durchführt und die gewonnenen Erkenntnisse als neue Testfälle für die Methodenbewertung und -ausmusterung speichert.", accept: "Die Komponente generiert innerhalb einer Woche mindestens fünf neue, valide Bestehenskriterien, die zuvor nicht abgedeckt waren, und markiert mindestens zwei Methoden als potenziell ruhestandsfähig."

But careful: the source explicitly says it's for coding agents, building a shadow knowledge base, and that agents do experiments on underexplored parts. It can detect bugs and ideate features. That directly maps to generating new test criteria (by discovering bugs) and retirement decisions (by finding deprecated code). So I'll connect it to method-trialing.

Now, verify that it doesn't touch core. The method-trialing module is non-core, and the change is to add a subcomponent that runs during idle time. That's fine.

Thus, output JSON.
